pattern: Rebuild the row-major grid from the flat string, then read it back off along every downward-right diagonal in column-start order to undo the slant, trimming the trailing padding at the end.
hints: The number of columns is fixed by the total length divided by the row count, since the matrix the text was written into is always fully rectangular, padding included.

For each starting column, walk one cell down and one cell right at a time — row 0 of that column, row 1 of the next column, and so on — collecting characters until you run off the bottom or the right edge.

Do that walk once per starting column in left-to-right order and concatenate everything collected; only the very end can carry leftover padding, so a single trailing trim recovers the original text exactly.

edge_inputs.description: three-row grid mixing a reversed-diagonal read across four columns
a four-row grid decoding a short multi-word phrase
a single row is the identity case: every diagonal is exactly one cell long
an empty ciphertext with an arbitrary row count must decode to an empty string
only one column exists, so every row past the first is entirely unreachable padding
the original text itself contains a space, which is indistinguishable in the grid from padding but still decodes correctly
a longer phrase spanning several rows and columns with padding scattered through the middle and the end
input: h lfo ewdo  lo    lr    o

edge_inputs.description: the grid is packed edge to edge with no padding at all, so trimming removes nothing
input: aglpsu bhmqt  cinr   djo    ek     f

stress.description: random valid encodings built from a random lowercase phrase (with occasional internal spaces) and a random row count
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(0, max(1, min(size, 2000)))
    rows = rng.randint(1, 50)
    letters = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'
    if n == 0:
        original = ''
    else:
        chars = []
        for i in range(n):
            if 0 < i < n - 1 and rng.random() < 0.1:
                chars.append(' ')
            else:
                chars.append(rng.choice(letters))
        while chars and chars[-1] == ' ':
            chars[-1] = rng.choice(letters)
        original = ''.join(chars)
    m = len(original)
    cols = 0
    capacity = 0
    while capacity < m:
        cols += 1
        capacity += min(rows, cols)
    cols = max(cols, 1)
    grid = [[' '] * cols for _ in range(rows)]
    idx = 0
    for d in range(cols):
        r, c = 0, d
        while r < rows and c < cols and idx < m:
            grid[r][c] = original[idx]
            idx += 1
            r += 1
            c += 1
    encoded = ''.join(''.join(row) for row in grid)
    return [encoded, rows]
stress.note: builds a valid encoding directly (minimal column count, diagonal fill) rather than relying on the solution under test
